name: Transform QuestionnaireResponse EQ-5D-5L to OMOP Measurement

data.name: get_fhir_data
data.sqlquery:
select qr.content
from 
fhir."QuestionnaireResponse" qr 
join fhir."Project" p 
on qr."projectId" = p."projectId" 
where p."name" = 'daa69b8b-3044-4fba-af44-08215b108e69'
data.description: Get QuestionnaireResponse fhir data from database

data.name: transform_fhir_to_omop
data.description: Transforms fhir data to omop measurement using structure map

data.name: insert_into_omop_table
data.description: Insert the records into omop measurement table

data.name: py2table
data.description: Describe the task of node py2table_node_0

data.name: convert_to_tabular_dataframe
data.description: Convert column wise data to tabular
data.python_code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd


# Concept ID for eq_vas — only this dimension uses value_as_number
EQ_VAS_CONCEPT_ID = 42537274


def flatten_measurements(df):
    """
    Flatten a DataFrame where some columns contain lists.

    Args:
      df: pandas.DataFrame produced from the mapping stage (may contain list-valued columns).

    Behavior:
      - For each row, expand any list-valued columns into multiple rows (aligned by index).
      - Skip any flattened row where `measurement_concept_id` is empty (None/NaN/non-convertible).
      - Set `value_as_number` only when `measurement_concept_id == 42537274` (eq_vas).
      - Assign a unique sequential measurement_id to every output row.
    """
    all_rows = []

    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        # Capture row-level scalar value_as_number before list expansion
        row_vas = row.get("value_as_number", None)
        if isinstance(row_vas, list):
            # If it somehow arrives as a list, take the first non-None value
            row_vas = next((v for v in row_vas if v is not None), None)

        # Determine maximum list length in this row
        max_len = max(
            (len(val) for val in row if isinstance(val, list)),
            default=1
        )

        for i in range(max_len):
            entry = {}
            for col, val in row.items():
                if col == "measurement_id":
                    # Skip — we assign measurement_id globally after flattening
                    continue
                if isinstance(val, list):
                    entry[col] = val[i] if i < len(val) else None
                else:
                    entry[col] = val

            # ── Normalize measurement_concept_id ──────────────────────────
            meas = entry.get("measurement_concept_id", None)

            if isinstance(meas, float) and np.isnan(meas):
                meas = None

            if isinstance(meas, (float, np.floating)):
                try:
                    meas = int(meas)
                except Exception:
                    meas = None

            # Skip rows with no concept ID (unmapped items)
            if meas is None:
                continue

            entry["measurement_concept_id"] = int(meas)

            # ── value_as_number: only for eq_vas ──────────────────────────
            entry["value_as_number"] = (
                row_vas if entry["measurement_concept_id"] == EQ_VAS_CONCEPT_ID else None
            )

            all_rows.append(entry)

    result_df = pd.DataFrame(all_rows)

    # ── Assign unique sequential measurement_id ───────────────────────────
    # Starting from 1; in production offset by a base value per patient/QR
    result_df["measurement_id"] = range(1, len(result_df) + 1)

    # Ensure correct column order with measurement_id first
    cols = ["measurement_id"] + [c for c in result_df.columns if c != "measurement_id"]
    result_df = result_df[cols]

    return result_df.reset_index(drop=True)


def exec(myinput):
    data = myinput.get("transform_fhir_to_omop").result
    df = flatten_measurements(data)
    return df
